title: QuadRF can spot drones and see WiFi through my wall
summary:
The QuadRF is a handheld, open-source phased-array radio system built around a Raspberry Pi 5 and an FPGA. Developed by Martin McCormick—a former SpaceX engineer who worked on Starlink’s "Dishy" terminal—the device brings advanced beamforming and signal processing capabilities to the open-source community.

A primary technical innovation of the QuadRF is its use of the Raspberry Pi 5’s MIPI (camera/display) lanes for data transfer. By repurposing these lanes, the device can stream I/Q data at speeds exceeding 5 Gbps with lower latency than traditional USB connections. Operating in the 4.9–6 GHz range, the tool allows users to track drones in flight and "see" WiFi signals through walls. It includes a custom Augmented Reality (AR) visualizer that represents RF frequencies as colorful "blobs" on a mobile interface.

The hardware is designed for scalability; while the handheld unit is intended for local SDR applications, multiple modules can be daisy-chained together. This modularity is part of a larger project aimed at Earth-Moon-Earth (EME) radio experiments and radio astronomy, capable of reaching a massive 1.15 MW EIRP.

Though the current prototype features a somewhat unrefined VNC-based user interface and a 3D-printed shell, the author was impressed by its performance and ability to pick up a DJI Mini Pro 4 drone from a distance. The QuadRF is currently available for pre-order on Crowd Supply for $499, with future production units moving toward injection-molded enclosures. Ultimately, the project demonstrates that sophisticated RF surveillance and analysis tools once reserved for governments are now accessible to hobbyists.
chinese_title: QuadRF可以穿墙发现无人机并探测WiFi信号
chinese_summary:
QuadRF 是一款基于 Raspberry Pi 5 和 FPGA 构建的手持式开源相控阵无线电系统。该设备由前 SpaceX 工程师 Martin McCormick 开发（他曾参与 Starlink “Dishy”终端的研发），为开源社区带来了先进的波束成形和信号处理能力。

QuadRF 的一项主要技术创新是利用了 Raspberry Pi 5 的 MIPI（摄像头/显示器）通道进行数据传输。通过复用这些通道，该设备能够以超过 5 Gbps 的速率传输 I/Q 数据，且延迟低于传统的 USB 连接。该工具运行在 4.9–6 GHz 频段，允许用户追踪飞行中的无人机并“透视”墙壁看到 WiFi 信号。它还包含一个自定义的增强现实（AR）可视化器，可在移动界面上将射频频率呈现为彩色的“色块”。

该硬件专为可扩展性而设计；虽然手持单元旨在用于本地 SDR 应用，但多个模块可以串联在一起。这种模块化设计是一个更大项目的一部分，旨在进行地-月-地（EME）无线电实验和射电天文学研究，其等效全向辐射功率（EIRP）可达惊人的 1.15 MW。

尽管目前的工程原型采用了尚不成熟的 VNC 用户界面和 3D 打印外壳，但作者对其性能以及远距离捕获 DJI Mini Pro 4 无人机的能力印象深刻。QuadRF 目前已在 Crowd Supply 平台上开启预售，价格为 499 美元，未来的量产单元将采用注塑成型外壳。该项目最终证明，曾经仅限政府使用的复杂射频监测与分析工具，现在普通爱好者也能触手可及。

title: Snails' Teeth Beats Spider Silk as Nature's Strongest Material (2015)
summary:
According to research published in the *Journal of the Royal Society Interface*, the teeth of marine snails known as limpets have been identified as the strongest natural material on Earth. This discovery displaces spider silk as the previous record-holder for biological strength.

Limpets use a tooth-studded tongue called a radula to grind down rock for food. Scientists found that these teeth are composed of iron-containing goethite nanofibers encased in a protein matrix. This unique structure provides a tensile strength—the ability to resist pulling apart—that is approximately five times greater than that of most spider silk. The material is comparable to high-quality carbon fiber and can withstand pressures that would normally turn carbon into diamond. To illustrate this, lead author Asa Barber noted that the strength is equivalent to a single strand of spaghetti holding up 3,300 pounds of sugar.

While the material does not surpass human-made graphene, it outperforms Kevlar and matches the strength of advanced synthetic fibers. The article also distinguishes between tensile strength and hardness, noting that while rare materials like lonsdaleite and wurtzite boron nitrate are harder than diamonds (resistant to scratching), limpet teeth represent the peak of natural resistance to breaking under tension.

These findings are of significant interest to the engineering community. By studying the microscopic structure of limpet teeth, researchers hope to develop bio-inspired materials for use in high-performance structures, machinery, and next-generation manufacturing.
chinese_title: 蜗牛牙齿超越蜘蛛丝，成为自然界最强韧的材料 (2015)
chinese_summary:
根据发表在《英国皇家学会界面杂志》上的研究，一种被称为“帽贝”的海蜗牛牙齿已被确认为地球上最强的天然材料。这一发现取代了此前的纪录保持者——蜘蛛丝，成为生物强度的新标杆。

帽贝利用一种长满牙齿、被称为“齿舌”的器官来磨碎岩石以获取食物。科学家发现，这些牙齿由包裹在蛋白质基质中的含铁针铁矿纳米纤维组成。这种独特的结构提供了极高的抗拉强度（即抵抗拉断的能力），约为大多数蜘蛛丝的五倍。该材料可与高质量碳纤维相媲美，并能承受通常使碳转化为钻石的巨大压力。为了形象地说明这一点，研究首席作者阿萨·巴伯（Asa Barber）指出，这种强度相当于一根意大利面条能吊起3300磅重的白糖。

虽然这种材料尚未超越人造石墨烯，但其性能优于凯夫拉纤维，并能与先进的合成纤维并驾齐驱。文中还区分了抗拉强度与硬度，指出尽管蓝丝黛尔石和纤锌矿型氮化硼等稀有材料比钻石更硬（即更耐刮擦），但帽贝牙齿代表了自然界抗拉伸破坏能力的最高水平。

这些发现引起了工程界的广泛关注。通过研究帽贝牙齿的微观结构，研究人员希望开发出仿生材料，并将其应用于高性能结构、机械设备以及下一代制造业。
